strategy: Round 1 he is under an Invulnerability aura - you cannot meaningfully damage him until it is gone, so spend the opening clearing minions.

The three Titan Experiments feed him Shadow Pips based on your casts: Traps (Death Titan 07), Blades/Shields (Storm Titan 291), and Globals (Life Titan 34). At 7 shadow pips he fires a large Titan AoE at the team. Minimize those cast types and kill the Titan that punishes your setup first.
Each minion death also triggers a 450 Shadow AoE on all wizards - heal and shield through the clear.
Once all minions are dead he swaps his aura for Frenzy, spiking his damage, so be set up to burst him fast after the board is clear.
Team: pick a hitter whose blades are minimal, clear Titans while managing pip-feeding, then unload before his Frenzy window hurts.
cheatNotes: Round 1 he puts an Invulnerability aura on himself - damage barely lands until it is replaced. Clear minions first.
Every time a minion dies, the Titan hits all wizards for 450 Shadow. Shield the team as you clear.
When the Rat reaches 7 Shadow Pips he spends his turn on a large Titan AoE against the whole team. Do not let his pips reach 7.
Once all minions are defeated he replaces his aura with Frenzy, spiking his damage. Be ready to burst him.
Casting a Trap makes Titan Experiment 07 give the Rat a Shadow Pip. Limit traps.
Casting a Blade or Shield makes Titan Experiment 291 give the Rat a Shadow Pip. Limit blades and shields.
Casting a Global makes Titan Experiment 34 give the Rat a Shadow Pip. Avoid globals.
